QF.id: Income, Residential Status, Five Heads of Income_18

question.text: Under the old tax regime, what is the maximum amount of loss from house property that can be set off against other heads of income in a given assessment year?

answer.text: No limit — the entire loss can be set off

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under section 71, the loss from house property that can be set off against other heads of income (like salary, PGBP, etc.) is limited to Rs.2,00,000 in a given assessment year under the old tax regime. Any excess loss beyond Rs.2 lakh can be carried forward for 8 assessment years for set-off against future house property income only. **The Trap:** Candidates confuse the Rs.2 lakh interest deduction cap for self-occupied property with the Rs.2 lakh set-off limit. While related, they are distinct provisions. Also, under the new tax regime, set-off of house property loss against other heads is NOT allowed (except deemed let-out loss against other house property income). **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM X-B frequently tests inter-head set-off limits, especially the distinction between old and new regime treatment. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients with multiple properties generating losses should plan under the old regime where the Rs.2 lakh set-off is available. Under the new regime, this benefit disappears entirely for most scenarios.
